zh.lifeSummary: 當Henry Rice Wade於1545年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾克利夫蘭時，他的父親Robert Wade45歲，母親Elizabeth31歲。他於1562年與Elizabeth Ramsden在英國英格蘭韋斯特 越可爾哈利法可結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和10個女兒的父母。他去世於1605年7月28日在他的家鄉享年60歲，埋葬在英國越可爾哈利法可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Henry Rice Wade于1545年出生在英国英格兰越可爾克利夫蘭时，他的父亲Robert Wade45岁，母亲Elizabeth31岁。他于1562年与Elizabeth Ramsden在英国英格兰韋斯特 越可爾哈利法可结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和10个女儿的父母。他去世于1605年7月28日在他的家乡享年60岁，埋葬在英国越可爾哈利法可。

nameMeanings.name: Wade
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Wade (Old English Wada or, via Norman French, from ancient Germanic Wado, of unclear origin but possibly related to Old English wadan ‘to go’).

English: topographic name from Middle English wade ‘ford’ (Old English (ge)wæd ‘ford, especially a tidal crossing’) for someone who lived by a ford, or a habitational name from a place so called, such as Wade Hall in North Cove (Suffolk).
Irish: shortened and altered from Mac Uaid, see McQuaid .

variants.name: Wadeson
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name   Wade  .

variants.name: Wass
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Wace (ancient Germanic Waz(z)o), a pet form of any of various ancient Germanic personal names beginning with Wad- (compare Wade 1). The name Wace was common among the Normans, alongside the Parisian French variant Gace, and was latinized as Wazo (nominative), Wazonis (genitive).
English (of Norman origin): occasionally, perhaps a variant of Vause with vocalizing of initial V-.
German: nickname from Middle High German was(se) ‘sharp, cutting’.

variants.name: Waide
meanings: Irish: shortened form of Mac Uaid, see McQuaid .

variants.name: Waddy
meanings: English: variant of Wadey, perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Wady, a pet form of Wade , or in northern England alternatively a shortened form of Waldo . The name may also be a variant of Wilday .

variants.name: McQuaid
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Colum, Nuala.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uaid ‘son of Uad’, Gaelic form of the personal name Wat, a short form of Walter . Compare the Scottish form McQuate .

variants.name: Waithe
meanings: either a topographic name from Old Norse vath ‘ford’, or a habitational name from a place so named, such as Waithe (Lincolnshire) and Wath (North and Yorkshire).

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

nameMeanings.name: Rice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Richard , or, less frequently, of Frederick or other names ending in -ric(k). It is also used as an independent given name, especially in North America.

variants.name: Richard
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Henry Rice Wade was born in 1545, in Cleveland, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Robert Wade, was 45 and his mother, Elizabeth, was 31. He married Elizabeth Ramsden in 1562, in Halifax, West Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 10 daughters. He died on 28 July 1605, in his hometown, at the age of 60, and was buried in Halifax, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Henry Rice Wade est né en 1545, à Cleveland, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert Wade, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Ramsden en 1562, à Halifax, West Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 10 filles. Il est décédé le 28 juillet 1605, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterré à Halifax, Everwyck, Angleterre.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Henry Rice Wade è nato il nel 1545 nel Cleveland, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Robert Wade, aveva 45 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Ramsden nel 1562, nel Halifax, West Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 10 figlie. Morì il 28 luglio 1605, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 60 anni, e fu sepolto a Halifax, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Henry Rice Wadeが1545年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアクレベランドで生まれた時，父Robert Wadeは45歳，母Elizabethは31歳であった。1562年にイギリスイギリスウエスト ヨークシュアイアハリファックスでElizabeth Ramsdenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と10人の娘の親であった。彼は1605年7月28日に故郷で60歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアハリファックスにて埋葬された。

ko.Halifax, West Riding of Yorkshire, Engla: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 할이파스 할이파스 스트 자메스
ko.King Crosse, Halifax, West Riding, Yorks: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 할이파스 할이파스 스트 자메스
ko.lifeSummary: Henry Rice Wade(이)가 1545년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 클레벨랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Wade(은)는 45세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1562년 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트 요르크실르 할이파스에서 Elizabeth Ramsden(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 열 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1605년 7월 28일 그의 고향에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 할이파스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Henry Rice Wade nasceu em 1545, em Cleveland, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Robert Wade, tinha 45 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Ramsden em 1562, em Halifax, West Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 10 filhas. Ele faleceu em 28 de julho de 1605, em sua cidade natal, com 60 anos, e foi sepultado em Halifax, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.Halifax, West Riding of Yorkshire, Engla: хэлифакс ст джемесь, хэлифакс, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.King Crosse, Halifax, West Riding, Yorks: хэлифакс ст джемесь, хэлифакс, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Henry Rice Wade родился в 1545 г. в местности кливеланд, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Robert Wade было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Ramsden в 1562 г. в местности хэлифакс, вест еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 10 дочерей. Он умер 28 июля 1605 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 60 лет и был похоронен в местности хэлифакс, еркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Henry Rice Wade nació en 1545, en Cleveland, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Robert Wade, tenía 45 años y su mamá, Elizabeth, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Ramsden en 1562, en Halifax, West Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 10 hijas. Murió el 28 de julio de 1605, en su pueblo, a la edad de 60 años, y fue sepultado en Halifax, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
